title: Redesign the header: fixed chrome, menu-driven nav, settings/user menu
description: Per `plano-de-interface.md`'s `# Header` section and the reference screenshots, the header must become pure navigation between large areas — never edit/admin actions — with the primary center nav driven by the new **Menu** entity (TASK-83/84: e.g. 'Get started / Foundation / Components / Resources'), not by `sections` and not by app routes. This supersedes the original plan of using `sections.list` directly for the header nav — confirmed with the user that Menu is a real new top-level entity, and clicking a menu item changes which menu is 'active', which in turn scopes the sidebar (TASK-86) to that menu's sections. Confirmed with the user: the current admin-only links (Usuários, Tokens, Histórico global, Página inicial settings) move into a settings/user menu on the right, alongside search. `AdminLayout.tsx` currently renders a static nav (Início/Histórico/Página inicial/Usuários/Tokens) — this task replaces it.
acceptanceCriteria: Header is a fixed ~64px bar with: left = logo/mark + project name; center = primary nav populated from `menus.list` (TASK-84), each item selecting that menu as 'active' — mirrors the plan's 'Get started / Foundation / Components' example using real menu titles

Some notion of 'active menu' is established and shared with the sidebar (TASK-86): either URL-driven (e.g. `/menus/:menuId/...` prefix) or lifted app state — pick whichever fits the existing router structure with the least disruption to current page/tab routes (`/pages/:pageId`, `/pages/:pageId/tabs/:tabId`), and document the choice since TASK-86/87 depend on it
Clicking a menu item with zero sections yet lands on an empty state (coordinate with TASK-90's empty-state work) rather than erroring; the header itself never blocks on a menu having content

An 'Add' affordance exists near the menu nav (per the plan's reference showing a '+ Add' control next to the top nav) for creating a new menu inline — reuses `menus.create` (TASK-84); renaming/reordering/deleting a menu is exposed via a lightweight per-item control (e.g. hover/click reveal, consistent with the ⋮-menu pattern TASK-89 establishes for sidebar items — reuse that pattern here rather than inventing a second one)

Right side holds: a search entry point (wired in TASK-91, a disabled/placeholder trigger is acceptable here if TASK-91 hasn't landed yet), a settings/gear affordance, and a user menu (avatar/initials + role) that opens a dropdown containing: Usuários (admin-only), Tokens (admin-only), Histórico global, Página inicial (settings), and Sair — everything currently in the flat nav plus the existing role-gating from `AdminLayout.tsx` preserved exactly

The header contains no edit/create/delete actions for **content** (pages/tabs/blocks) — per the plan's 'Nunca colocar ações de edição nele' — menu management is the one deliberate, narrowly-scoped exception, since menus are themselves top-level navigation structure, not content
Active-menu highlighting in the center nav uses the same subtle style already used elsewhere (no heavy borders/pills)
All existing routes (`/admin/history`, `/admin/settings/landing-page`, `/admin/users`, `/admin/settings/tokens`, `/login` logout flow) remain reachable and functionally unchanged
Uses the typography/token primitives from TASK-82

steps.details: Compare a URL-based approach (menu id/slug in the route) against React state/context. A URL-based approach is recommended for shareable links and consistency with the rest of the app's routing (pages/tabs are already URL-addressed) — but confirm it composes cleanly with existing `/pages/:pageId` routes (a page belongs to exactly one section, which belongs to exactly one menu, so the active menu can often be *derived* from the current page without needing it in the URL at all — evaluate this derivation approach too before adding new URL segments).
steps.resolution: Chose lifted React state (`useActiveMenu` hook in AppHeader.tsx, owned by AdminLayout), not a URL segment. Rationale: the sidebar (SidebarTree.tsx) still queries the unscoped `sections.list` today — TASK-86 is what switches it to `sections.listByMenu` and is where page→section→menu derivation and URL-vs-page-driven consistency actually matters. Adding a route segment now would touch `/pages/:pageId` routes for no consumer yet. `activeMenuId` defaults to the first menu from `menus.list` once loaded and is passed down via `Outlet context={{ me, activeMenuId }}` for TASK-86/87 to read (and `setActiveMenuId` is available in AdminLayout to pass down further if needed). Documented inline in AppHeader.tsx's `useActiveMenu` doc comment.

steps.description: Build the menu-driven center nav + inline menu management
steps.details: Query `menus.list`; render each as a nav item with active-state; add the '+' create-menu affordance and per-item rename/reorder/delete (reusing TASK-89's contextual-menu component once available, or building the minimal version here if sequencing requires it — coordinate ordering with TASK-89).
steps.resolution: Built in AppHeader.tsx (MenuNav/MenuNavItem/AddMenu). TASK-89 hadn't landed yet, so a minimal per-item shadcn DropdownMenu ('⋮' via MoreHorizontal icon, visible on hover/focus) was built here with Renomear/Mover para cima/Mover para baixo/Excluir, wired to menus.rename/reorder/delete. Rename reuses the same inline-edit-in-place pattern as SidebarTree's NodeRow. Flagged for TASK-89 to extract into a shared RowActionsMenu component per its own technicalNotes.

steps.details: Add a shadcn `DropdownMenu` (or equivalent) triggered by the avatar/role chip, containing the relocated links with the same `me.data.role === 'admin'` gating already in `AdminLayout.tsx`, plus the existing Sair/logout mutation.
steps.resolution: UserMenu component in AppHeader.tsx: shadcn DropdownMenu (added via `npx shadcn add dropdown-menu avatar`) triggered by an avatar+role chip; a separate gear icon button opens the same controlled dropdown as a second entry point (matches the plan's 3-icon 'busca · configurações · usuário' right side while keeping a single item list). Contains Histórico global, Página inicial, Usuários (admin-only), Tokens (admin-only), Sair — same role gating as before.

steps.details: Replace the current flat `<nav>` with the new fixed header (logo/name left, menu nav center, search+settings+user right). Preserve the `me` auth-gate/loading/redirect logic untouched.
steps.resolution: AdminLayout.tsx now renders `<AppHeader>` (fixed, h-16, inset-x-0) above a `pt-20` body wrapper containing the unchanged sidebar+Outlet. auth.me loading/redirect logic untouched. Logo/name links to `/` so the old 'Início' entry stays reachable.

steps.details: Confirm every relocated link still works (Usuários/Tokens admin-only, Histórico global/Página inicial for both roles), logout still works, menu nav items select correctly with 0/1/many menus, and creating/renaming/deleting a menu from the header works end-to-end including cascade behavior on delete.
steps.resolution: No Chrome browser extension available in this session, so verified via: `pnpm --filter @systembook/admin typecheck/lint/build` all clean; and an end-to-end tRPC client probe (using the app's own `@trpc/client` + httpBatchLink, against the real dev server) exercising the exact calls the UI makes — auth.login, auth.me, menus.list/create/rename/reorder/delete — all succeeded and left the DB back at just the original backfilled menu. Relocated links (`/admin/history`, `/admin/settings/landing-page`, `/admin/users`, `/admin/settings/tokens`) are unchanged route paths, only moved into the DropdownMenu via react-router `Link`, so routing itself is untouched. Recommend a manual click-through once Chrome tooling is available, but no code-level risk was found.

technicalNotes: This task's scope grew from the original plan once Menu was confirmed as a real new entity (not a rename of Section) — it now owns both the header chrome AND the minimum menu-management UI, since menus are created/organized from the header, not the sidebar. Budget accordingly; consider whether menu rename/delete strictly needs to ship in this task or can follow immediately after in a fast-follow, but menu **create** must work here since TASK-86 (sidebar) needs at least one real menu to scope against beyond the TASK-83 backfilled default.

The public documentation site (`/docs/...`) is explicitly untouched by the Menu feature — menus are an admin-only organizational layer for now (same 'admin-only' framing as Fase 9). `sections.listPublic` (TASK-83's schema change doesn't require touching it) continues to group only by section; whether the public reader ever sees a menu-level nav is an open question for a future phase, not this one.
Search trigger stays a stub here (TASK-91's scope).

SidebarTree.tsx was deliberately left untouched (still queries the unscoped `sections.list` and creates sections against `LEGACY_DEFAULT_MENU_ID`) — that migration to `sections.listByMenu` + the header's `activeMenuId` is TASK-86's scope, not this one.

New files: `apps/admin/src/features/navigation/AppHeader.tsx` (AppHeader + useActiveMenu, exported for AdminLayout/TASK-86), `apps/admin/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx` and `avatar.tsx` (shadcn, first use of both in this project).
